title: Hedge
author: Jane Delury
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Hedge" book by Jane Delury?
Options:
A. Maud was pleased as she went past the pond again. Neither Frazer nor Chris had said anything about the slippers. She was back with her people. Gardening didn't require you to be sociable. You didn't have to worry about how you were dressed or if you were being clever. You didn't have to do what she had been doing the last two years in Marin - chatting with the wives of Peter's business partners at cocktail parties while trying not to fall over in high heels. Even crabby Harriet, who now stood on the porch with the pitcher of lemonade and a grumpy look on her face - she must have seen how Maud left the archives - was a relief.  
B. Maud smiled as she passed the pond again. Neither Frazer nor Chris had said anything about the slippers. She was back with her people. You didnt have to be sociable in gardening. You didnt have to think about how you dressed or whether you were being clever. You didnt have to do what she had been doing for the past two years in Marin, chatting with the wives of Peters partners at cocktail parties as she tried to balance in heels. Even grouchy Harriet, who now stood on the porch with the pitcher of lemonade and an aggrieved look on her faceshe must have seen how Maud left the archiveswas a relief.
C. Maud was happy as she walked by the pond another time. Neither Frazer nor Chris had commented on the slippers. She was reunited with her loved ones. You didn't need to be friendly while gardening. You didn't need to think about how you looked or if you were being smart. You didn't need to do what she had been doing the past two years in Marin - making small talk with the wives of Peter's coworkers at cocktail parties as she tried to stay balanced in heels. Even grumpy Harriet, who now stood on the porch with the pitcher of lemonade and an annoyed expression on her face - she must have noticed how Maud left the archives - was a comfort.
D. Maud was happy as she walked by the pond once more. Neither Frazer nor Chris had mentioned the slippers. She was reunited with her community. Gardening didn't require sociability. You didn't need to ponder your outfit or wit. You didn't need to do what she had done the past two years in Marin - making small talk with the spouses of Peter's colleagues at cocktail parties while balancing in heels. Even irritable Harriet, who now stood on the porch with lemonade and a bothered expression - she must have noticed Maud leave the archives - was reassuring.
Answer:

Even crabby Harriet, who now stood on the porch with the pitcher of lemonade and a grumpy look on her face - she must have seen how Maud left the archives - was a relief.
